_instructions: This file draws the 'Blacksmith Fork River' schematic page (the entire Blacksmith Fork system, its own standalone page).
See public/schematics/README.md for the full field reference and a longer walkthrough - this is the quick version.
NODES: each node is a card on the diagram.
id   - unique within this file, used by edges below to connect nodes.
name - for type 'station', must fuzzy-match a live station's display name from HydroServer/DWRi/USGS.
row  - vertical position, smaller = further upstream/higher on the page.
col  - horizontal lane. This page is a simple single-channel chain, so everything uses col 2 today; add col 1/3
if a tributary or diversion is added later, same convention as the other two pages.
type - 'station' (shows live data), 'junction' (invisible connection point, no card), or 'extension' (a card that
navigates to a different schematic page when clicked). This page has no 'terminus' node - it flows out
via the 'to_lower_logan' extension node instead, since Blacksmith Fork joins the main Logan River channel
rather than ending at Cutler Reservoir directly.
colorGroup - optional; must match a name from waterway_groups or schematic_groups in config/station_config.yaml.
EDGES: 'style' is 'main' (thick dark line) for this page's single chain; 'direction' defaults to 'in' and isn't
needed here since there are no diversions/canals yet.
To add a new station: add a node with type 'station', then add an edge connecting it into the chain at the right point.
The 'to_lower_logan' node at the bottom is the reciprocal of the 'bsf_ext' extension node on lower-logan.json - do not
title: Blacksmith Fork River
subtitle: The full Blacksmith Fork River system

nodes.name: Blacksmith Fork River: Before Confluence with Logan River
